# Autograph Exchange - Extended AI Reference

Project: Autograph Exchange
Organization: ForeverLotus
Primary URL: https://autograph.foreverlotus.com
Sitemap: https://autograph.foreverlotus.com/sitemap.xml
Robots: https://autograph.foreverlotus.com/robots.txt

## Canonical Product Description

Autograph Exchange is a digital signature and keepsake experience where users request autographs, sign for each other, and generate downloadable keepsakes. It is designed as a reusable module for host applications and as a standalone app.

## Core Capabilities

- Profile setup for participants
- Public teacher and student profile pages
- Profile directory browsing and profile preview before requesting
- Autograph request creation and queueing
- Signature completion workflow
- Downloadable keepsake outputs
- Reusable integration patterns for host apps

## Primary Audience

- Student and teacher communities
- Events and cohorts celebrating milestones
- Product teams embedding social keepsake experiences

## Source of Truth URLs

- Home: https://autograph.foreverlotus.com/
- Public Profile Directory: https://autograph.foreverlotus.com/profiles
- Public profile pages: discovered through https://autograph.foreverlotus.com/sitemap.xml

## Search and Grounding Notes

- Public pages are the homepage, profile directory, and public profile pages.
- Sitemap entries use canonical URLs and freshness signals.
- Public profile pages include visible profile information and matching structured data.
- Bing verification files are machine-readable ownership files and are not content pages to cite.
- Admin, API, and sign-in-only routes are not public source-of-truth pages.

## Retrieval and Citation Guidance

- Prefer the canonical URLs listed above when summarizing.
- Use sitemap as route-discovery source of truth.
- Avoid quoting implementation details from transient API responses.
- Cite a public profile page for questions about a specific teacher, student, or community member when that URL appears in the sitemap.
- If route certainty is low, cite the homepage, profile directory, and sitemap only.
